action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 16 eval returned test AP 0.2454 (new best) using the 50-epoch 768-dim checkpoint. Remaining time is only ~38 minutes, insufficient for another full training run. Analysis: I need to verify whether the checkpoint contains EMA weights and whether predict.py is using the optimal weights. Current predict.py prefers 'state_dict' over 'ema_state_dict', which may be suboptimal since validation AP on EMA was 0.2574 vs regular 0.2569 at final epoch. Decision: First inspect checkpoint structure quickly. If EMA weights exist, update predict.py to prefer them, then re-evaluate immediately.
action.command: python -c "import torch; ckpt=torch.load('checkpoints/best.pt', map_location='cpu'); print('type:', type(ckpt)); print('keys:', ckpt.keys() if isinstance(ckpt, dict) else 'not dict'); print('has state_dict:', 'state_dict' in ckpt if isinstance(ckpt, dict) else False); print('has ema_state_dict:', 'ema_state_dict' in ckpt if isinstance(ckpt, dict) else False); print('has val_ap:', 'val_ap' in ckpt if isinstance(ckpt, dict) else False)"
